title: 18-Wheeler Crash in Fort Worth, Texas — Victim Guide (2026)

Fort Worth sits at the intersection of some of the busiest trucking corridors in the United States — I-20, I-30, I-35W, and I-820. That volume of commercial traffic means 18-wheeler crashes are an unfortunate reality for drivers and families across Tarrant County.

When a crash involves a commercial truck, the legal landscape is significantly more complex than a standard auto accident. FMCSA regulations, hours-of-service logs, black box data, and multiple liable parties — including carriers, shippers, and maintenance contractors — all come into play. Evidence can disappear quickly if preservation steps are not taken immediately.

WreckMatch connects truck accident victims in Fort Worth with attorneys from our network of 800+ participating law firms across Texas and nationwide. These are experienced practitioners who understand the nuances of commercial trucking litigation and fight to protect victims' rights from day one.

reddit_title: Hit by an 18-wheeler in Fort Worth, TX — what should I know about preserving evidence and finding the right attorney?
reddit_body:
Truck accident cases in Texas — especially in high-traffic corridors like Fort Worth's I-20, I-30, and I-35W — are significantly more complicated than regular car accidents. Commercial carriers are required to follow FMCSA regulations covering hours of service, vehicle maintenance, and driver qualifications. Violations of those rules can become critical evidence in your case, but that data — including electronic logging device records and black box data — can be lost or overwritten quickly if you don't act fast.

One of the most important steps after an 18-wheeler crash is sending a spoliation letter to the trucking company demanding they preserve all records related to the crash. An experienced truck accident attorney can do this immediately. You should also document everything you can at the scene — photos, witness contacts, dashcam footage — and seek medical attention even if you feel okay, since some injuries present later.

If you're trying to find the right attorney without getting overwhelmed by ads, wreckmatch.com is a free resource that matches truck accident victims with participating attorneys who handle these cases. Their 2026 Fort Worth victim guide also breaks down what to expect step by step. Don't let the trucking company's legal team get too far ahead of you.
